sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  less than 6 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working inside of a server cabinet replacing a light bulb. He was standing on the second rung of a 4-foot ladder when he slipped and fell. The employee sustained a fractured left ankle that required surgery.

sampleRecords.nature: Closed trauma involving internal organs, major blood vessels
sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was leaving the office when they tripped on carpet and fell to the ground. The employee sustained internal bleeding in the rib area.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cleaning off a pulley on a conveyor belt when the conveyor caught her right index finger, resulting in a fingertip amputation.

sampleRecords.nature: Cuts, lacerations, punctures without injury to internal structures
sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by rolling, sliding, or shifting objects non-running
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was helping install a drill rod into a boring machine. The rod slipped, lacerating the employee's right middle finger. The employee was hospitalized.
An employee was restarting a compressor when he bumped into the side of a staircase and suffered a bruised leg. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecalls.title: Royal Oak Enterprises Recalls Lighters Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Fire and Burn Hazards; Violates Mandatory Standard for Multipurpose Lighters
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Royal Oak flame saber lighters. The lighters are red with a black-and-gray checkered grip and have a hands-free operation lock. The lighter bears the warning labels, "DANGER: Extremely Flammable, Content Under Pressure" and "WARNING: Only store in locked position" on the sides of the lighter. The …
hazards: The recalled lighters violate the mandatory standard for multipurpose lighters because they do not have the required child-resistant mechanisms, posing a risk …

sampleRecalls.title: Bexco Enterprises Recalls Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Swivel Gliders with USB Port Due to Overheating Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Babyletto Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Glider fabric-covered chairs with two USB device charging ports. The chair is covered in various color fabrics and is approximately 29 inches wide by 36 inches deep and 41 inches high and weighs approximately 125 pounds. Units included in the recall include t…
hazards: The USB module in the recliner arm can overheat and spark, posing an overheating hazard.
injuries: The firm has received 37 reports of incidents of overheating, smoking or sparking including one minor injury. No fires or property damage have been reported.
products: Babyletto Kiwi Electronic Recliner and Swivel Gliders with USB port
units: About 9,030 (Additionally, about six were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: EVAS 20 lb. Propane Exchange Tanks Recalled by Worthington Enterprises Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinders with model number EVAS - M0859, a serial number beginning with a "W," and a date code of either "06-23" or "07-23." The model number, serial number, and date code are stamped on the collar of the cylinder. While the cylinders were initially sold with an Ameri…
hazards: The recalled propane exchange cylinders could leak gas, posing a fire hazard.

sampleRecalls.title: Trek Recalls Promax Hydraulic Disc Brakes Sold on Trek Bicycles Due to Crash Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Trek bicycles with revision 1 of the Promax Solve DSK-925 and Promax F1 DSK-927 hydraulic disc brakes installed as original equipment on the bicycle models listed in the table below. The serial number is printed on a sticker underneath the frame of the bicycle. Trek Bicycles Model Year 21: FX 2, V…
hazards: The brake hose can detach from the brake lever, posing a crash hazard.
injuries: Trek has received 195 reports of brake hoses detaching from the brake lever in the United States. No injuries have been reported.
products: Promax hydraulic disc brakes sold on Trek bicycles

sampleRecalls.title: Allied Air Enterprises Recalls Armstrong Air and Air Ease Gas Furnaces Due to Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves 96% efficiency gas furnaces under the ARMSTRONG and AIREASE brand names with the model numbers A96UH1E045B12S, A96UH1E070B12S, and/or A96UH1E110C20S. The furnaces weigh between 129 and 170 pounds and were sold between September 2022 and March 2023. The brand name is printed on the outside of the f…
hazards: A component inside the furnace was improperly assembled, causing the furnace to produce high levels of carbon monoxide (CO) under certain operating conditions,…
products: Armstrong Air and Air Ease single stage gas furnaces
